description: Extend the single-token genericity probe (e-0039) to multi-token MOTIFS (contiguous normalized-tactic n-grams, n=2,3). For each conceptual-bridge pair (the 200 MIN-closest cross-cluster proof pairs from edit_distance_bridges) and each random cross-cluster control pair from the same top-30 x top-30-longest selection, find SHARED contiguous n-grams and count those that are domain-specific (motif appears in <=3 of ~138 Leiden clusters AS A WHOLE) and, separately, 'emergent' motifs that are domain-specific despite every constituent token being generic (>=30 clusters) -- a-0036's exact hypothesis.
motivation: a-0036 measured genericity PER SINGLE TOKEN and a-0037 showed IDF-weighting single tokens does not recover conceptual bridges; but a-0036 explicitly flagged the untested escape hatch that 'longer domain-specific tactic MOTIFS assembled from individually-generic tokens are not captured -- a natural next probe.' Cheap CPU-only test over existing edit_distance_bridges + theorem_tactics + clusters data.
formal_results: 15,777 normalized proofs (>=5 tactics). n=2: 0/200 conceptual-bridge pairs share an EMERGENT motif (domain-specific motif built from all-generic tokens); only 2/200 share ANY domain-specific (<=3-cluster) motif (random control 0.0008, ~floor); mean shared motifs 0.145 (bridges) vs 0.0092 (random), ~16x. n=3: 0/200 emergent, 1/200 any domain-specific (random 0.0). The 1-2 'specific' motifs surfaced are themselves generic idioms ((gcongr, linarith only [?]); (rw[@,@,@], rw[@], rw[@])) that merely fall under <=3 clusters.
results_description: Conceptual-bridge proof pairs share MORE tactic motifs than random cross-cluster pairs (~16x, confirming the edit-distance similarity is real signal), but those shared motifs are essentially never domain-specific: 0/200 at both n=2 and n=3 are domain-specific motifs assembled from generic tokens, directly REFUTING a-0036's emergent-motif hypothesis.
conclusions: The motif probe a-0036 named does NOT rescue conceptual-bridge detection: multi-token tactic n-grams expose no domain-specific shared structure between conceptual-bridge pairs, at the random floor. This extends a-0037 from single tokens (weighted or not) to multi-token MOTIFS, definitively closing the normalized-tactic representation path: neither tokens nor motifs recover Brouwer<->Lawvere-style conceptual bridges. The conceptual signal is not in proof tactics at any n-gram order -- it is a corpus/representation limit (statement/type CONTENT, unexposed), confirming a-0037's diagnosis.
